Calculate adherence (batched processing)

Source: R/adherenceCalculations.R
calculateAdherenceBatched.Rd

Calculates adherence in batches for large datasets.

Usage

calculateAdherenceBatched(
  drugExposure,
  cdm,
  name = "adherenceFromOMOP_results",
  cma = c("CMA1", "CMA2", "CMA3", "CMA4", "CMA5", "CMA6", "CMA7", "CMA8", "CMA9"),
  medicationGroup = NULL,
  batchSize = 1000,
  ...
)

Arguments

drugExposure

Database table reference or data frame containing drug exposure data with patient information, typically generated by [generateChronicDrugExposure()]. Required columns: person_id, drug_concept_id, drug_exposure_start_date, days_supply.

cdm

(`cdm_reference`) A CDM reference object created with CDMConnector.

name

(`character(1)`) Name for the output table in the database.

cma

(`character`) Vector of CMA types to calculate. Options: `"CMA1"` through `"CMA9"`. See [AdhereR documentation](https://www.adherer.eu/) for details on each CMA type.

medicationGroup

(`list` or `NULL`) Named list where each element contains drug_concept_id values for a medication group. CMA is calculated separately for each group. If `NULL`, all medications are treated as one group.

batchSize

(`numeric(1)`) Number of subjects to process in each batch. Default: 1000. Increase for better performance with large datasets, decrease if running into memory issues.

...

Additional arguments passed to AdhereR CMA functions.

Value

Database table reference containing adherence calculations.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
cdm <- mockDrugExposure()
conceptSet <- CodelistGenerator::getDrugIngredientCodes(cdm, name = "Atorvastatin")

drugExposure <- generateChronicDrugExposure(
  cdm = cdm,
  conceptSet = conceptSet,
  name = "drug_exposure"
)

adherenceData <- drugExposure |>
  calculateAdherenceBatched(cdm, cma = "CMA5", name = "adherence_results")
} # }
